---
title: "Buoyant_Weighing_March2026"
author: "Chloé Gilligan"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:


```{r}
library(readr)
library(lme4)
library(car) 
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse) 
library(dplyr)
library(lmerTest)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(dunn.test)
```

# Load data
```{r}
tissue <- read_csv("../data/20260319_BuoyantWeighing_Measurements.csv")
```

# Clean + extract treatment info
```{r}
tissue <- tissue %>%
  rename(Sample_ID = `Sample ID`,
         Tissue_Weight = `Tissue weight`) %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_Code = str_extract(Sample_ID, "^[A-Z]{2}[A-Z]"),  # e.g. CRB
    Color = str_extract(Sample_Code, "[A-Z]$"),              # B, G, R, P
    Site = str_extract(Sample_Code, "^[A-Z]{2}"),            # CR, BR, RI
  )

tissue <- tissue %>%
  mutate(
    Tissue_Weight = as.numeric(Tissue_Weight)
  )

# Decode treatments (same logic as your AFDW script)
tissue <- tissue %>%
  mutate(
    Temperature = recode(Color,
      "B" = "Ambient",
      "G" = "Ambient",
      "R" = "Warming",
      "P" = "Warming"
    ),
    Risk = recode(Color,
      "B" = "Present",
      "G" = "Absent",
      "R" = "Present",
      "P" = "Absent"
    ),
    Temperature = factor(Temperature, levels = c("Ambient", "Warming")),
    Risk        = factor(Risk, levels = c("Absent", "Present"))
  )

```

# Remove bad values
```{r}
tissue <- tissue %>%
  filter(!is.na(Tissue_Weight)) %>%
  filter(Tissue_Weight > 0)
```


```{r}
#Plotting
# Color palette (same as before)
pal_temp <- c("Ambient" = "#1C86EE", "Warming" = "#EE0000")

p_tissue <- ggplot(tissue, aes(x = Risk, y = Tissue_Weight, color = Temperature)) +
  
  # Mean point
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean,
    geom = "point",
    size = 4,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)
  ) +
  
  # SE error bars
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_se,
    geom = "errorbar",
    width = 0.15,
    linewidth = 0.8,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)
  ) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_temp) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Absent" = "No Crab", "Present" = "Crab")) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Predation Risk",
    y = "Tissue Weight",
    color = "Temperature"
  ) +
  
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_tissue
```

```{r}
pal_temp <- c("Ambient" = "#1C86EE", "Warming" = "#EE0000")

p_tissue <- ggplot(tissue, aes(x = Risk, y = Tissue_Weight, color = Temperature)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 4,
               position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar",
               width = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8,
               position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_temp) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Absent" = "No Crab", "Present" = "Crab")) +
  labs(x = "Predation Risk", y = "Tissue Weight", color = "Temperature") +
  theme_classic()

p_tissue
```


```{r}
# Set contrasts
options(contrasts = c("contr.sum", "contr.poly"))

# Levene's test
leveneTest(Tissue_Weight ~ Temperature * Risk, data = tissue)
```


```{r}
# Random effect = Sample_Code 
m1_tissue <- lmer(Tissue_Weight ~ Temperature * Risk + (1|Sample_Code),
                  data = tissue, REML = TRUE)

# QQ plot
qqnorm(residuals(m1_tissue), main = "QQ Plot - Tissue Weight")
qqline(residuals(m1_tissue), col = "red", lwd = 2)

# ANOVA 
Anova(m1_tissue, test = "F", type = "III")

# Model summary
summary(m1_tissue)
```
